response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong foundations in software craftsmanship, event-driven systems, and distributed architectures, but the role's core requirement is deep, current C++ expertise — which is conspicuously absent from his profile. His only C++ exposure is a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a student-era audio synthesis project, neither of which demonstrates the STL mastery, multi-threaded system design, or secure C++ development the role demands. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js, and there is no evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge, IEC 62443 familiarity, MQTT, or industrial protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), making him a poor fit for this specific position despite his overall engineering maturity.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals — TDD, agile, CI/CD, trunk-based development applied consistently across multiple roles
Demonstrated experience with event-driven and high-performance distributed architectures (Sociomantic microservices at millions of HTTP requests/sec, GISCollective event-driven platform)
Breadth of engineering experience (15+ years) across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure, with evidence of mentoring and community contribution
concerns: C++ is not a current or primary skill; last meaningful use was ~10 years ago in a junior/staff role and a university thesis project — far below the role's required proficiency
No industrial cybersecurity exposure: IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or any industrial-protocol experience (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, DNP 3.0, MQTT) is entirely absent from the resume
No industrial automation or IoT domain background; the single relevant touchpoint (National Instruments, 7 months) was over a decade ago and focused on a C IDE, not platform engineering

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 knowledge
